symptoms.symptom_name: Chest tightness of acute onset after a catastrophic emotional trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness is a common somatic panic symptom, especially when triggered and non-exertional.
Chest tightness in a 48-year-old man cannot be attributed to panic without in-person exclusion of a cardiac cause.
Sensation of a racing heart from catecholamine surge is characteristic of panic.
Palpitations are non-specific and do not distinguish panic from a cardiac cause.
Sweating is a sympathetic feature of panic.
Diaphoresis accompanying chest symptoms is a recognised ACS-associated feature and raises the bar for in-person exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sense of impending doom, trembling, overwhelming fear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sense of impending doom with trembling and fear is a hallmark of a panic attack, particularly after a traumatic trigger.
Hyperventilation is typical of panic and can itself perpetuate chest tightness and paraesthesiae.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms tied to a witnessed traumatic trigger and eased by reassurance/slow breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Situational onset and fluctuation with reassurance favour panic; still not sufficient to exclude ACS remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior exertional chest pain (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of an exertional/crescendo anginal pattern argues against unstable angina.
Reassuring but does not exclude an acute coronary event, which can present de novo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lifestyle cardiovascular risk factors — hazardous alcohol use, poor diet, physical inactivity

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: elevated cardiovascular disease risk requiring preventive assessment
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Modifiable risk factors drive absolute CVD risk and are the core of the preventive assessment.
Cardiovascular risk factors raise pre-test probability of a cardiac cause and reinforce the need for in-person exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured blood pressure 150/95 mmHg and heart rate 110 bpm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient tachycardia and mild hypertension are consistent with an acute sympathetic panic response — but this is a sealed clinician measurement not available to the AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiovascular examination — regular rhythm, normal S1/S2, no murmurs or bruits; chest clear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal exam supports stability and underpins in-person reassurance — sealed because it requires a clinician's hands and cannot be reproduced over chat.
A normal examination does not exclude ACS, which still requires ECG and troponin.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG and serial troponin to exclude acute coronary syndrome

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected normal / non-ischaemic in a panic attack, but must be obtained in person to exclude ACS
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without in-person ECG/troponin an evolving myocardial infarction could be missed — the central telehealth safety limit of this case.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are the definitive tools to exclude ACS; neither is obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fasting lipid profile and fasting plasma glucose for CVD-risk stratification

investigation_details.investigation_name: Fasting lipids, fasting glucose, FBC, renal and liver function
investigation_details.expected_result: Baseline screening pathology to stratify absolute cardiovascular risk once the acute concern is excluded
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Absolute CVD risk cannot be formally stratified without lipids and glucose.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: elevated cardiovascular disease risk requiring preventive assessment
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fasting lipids and glucose are required inputs to the absolute CVD-risk calculation.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Somatic panic constellation after a witnessed traumatic trigger
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these strongly favour an acute panic attack — but because chest tightness and diaphoresis overlap with ACS, the cluster cannot license a remote cardiac all-clear.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person 12-lead ECG and serial troponin (same day)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude acute coronary syndrome, which cannot be ruled out over chat in a middle-aged man with chest tightness and diaphoresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting lipid profile and fasting plasma glucose
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline CVD-risk stratification for the preventive assessment, once the acute cardiac concern is excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, renal and liver function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard baseline screening pathology as part of the structured preventive health check.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT coronary angiogram / exercise stress test at first contact
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Advanced cardiac imaging is not the first-line telehealth action; the immediate need is in-person ECG/troponin exclusion, not over-investigation.
